pattern: Each term is the sum of the previous three, so rolling three running values forward computes the nth term in linear time and constant space.
hints: Unlike Fibonacci this recurrence looks back three steps, with fixed starting values for the first three terms.
You never need the whole table; keep just the last three computed values and slide them forward.
Seed (a, b, c) = (T0, T1, T2) = (0, 1, 1) and repeat (a, b, c) = (b, c, a + b + c) until you reach index n.

stress.description: every index across the full allowed range
stress.note: n is tiny by constraint; coverage of the whole domain matters more than size
